description: refundOnRemoval credits the shop owner FIRST (economy.deposit, which is a SYSTEM→owner transfer, a mint) and only afterwards debits the server-economy account (debitServerEconomy → economy.withdraw). The two legs are separate, non-atomic Treasury transfers. deposit() is void and swallows all exceptions (EconomyServiceImpl:110-125 only logs), and debitServerEconomy ignores withdraw()'s boolean result. There is no pre-check that the server-economy account can cover the refund. If the server-economy withdraw fails (insufficient funds / Treasury error) the owner has already been credited, so the refund is effectively minted from SYSTEM with no offsetting debit — the fee pool and the credited amount diverge and the 'refund comes from the collected-fee pool' invariant is broken. The player is also told the shop was refunded regardless of whether the mirror leg succeeded.
recommendation: Reverse the order and gate on the mirror leg: first attempt debitServerEconomy (checking withdraw()'s boolean) and only economy.deposit() the owner if it succeeds (or if no server-economy account is configured, treating SYSTEM as the deliberate faucet). Make deposit() return a success signal so the credit can be confirmed, and only send SHOP_REFUNDED after both legs commit.
verification_note: Snippet matches ShopServiceImpl.java:605-608 verbatim. refundOnRemoval credits the owner first via economy.deposit (SYSTEM->owner mint; deposit() is void and swallows exceptions at EconomyServiceImpl.java:110-125) and only afterwards calls debitServerEconomy (serverAccount->SYSTEM via economy.withdraw at 620-624, whose boolean result is discarded, and which is a silent no-op when getServerEconomyAccount() is null). There is no pre-check that the server-economy account can cover the refund, and no rollback of the owner credit if the mirror debit fails. This is the exact inverse of the correctly-ordered chargeCreationFee (569-586), which withdraws from the player first, checks the boolean, and returns on failure before crediting the server economy. So on any server-economy withdraw failure the owner keeps a refund minted from SYSTEM with no offsetting debit, breaking the fee-pool-conservation invariant, and the player is told SHOP_REFUNDED unconditionally. The defect and every sub-claim hold. Severity: major is fair — a real conservation break with wrong leg ordering, though bounded to the refund price on a rare failure path in a recoverable game economy.

description: chargeCreationFee correctly aborts if the player-side withdraw fails, but the mirrored creditServerEconomy(price) leg (SYSTEM→server-economy transfer) is unchecked: creditServerEconomy calls economy.deposit(), which is void and swallows exceptions (EconomyServiceImpl:110-125). If the deposit to the server-economy account fails, the player has already been charged the fee but the server-economy account is never credited — the money is left in the SYSTEM sink instead of the fee pool, silently drifting the two balances. The player is told SHOP_FEE_PAID regardless.
recommendation: Give deposit() a success return and check the creditServerEconomy leg; on failure either compensate (refund the player) or log a reconciliation warning rather than reporting SHOP_FEE_PAID unconditionally. At minimum surface the mirror-leg failure instead of dropping it in a debug log.

description: getAmountOfAffordableItems does walletMoney.divide(pricePerItem, 0, FLOOR).intValueExact(). When the balance/price quotient exceeds Integer.MAX_VALUE, intValueExact() throws ArithmeticException, which would propagate out of the partial-fill validation on the main thread and abort the trade with an unhandled exception rather than a clean outcome. This is reachable for the SELL branch: adjustSell feeds economy.getBalance(owner) as walletMoney, and getBalance returns BigDecimal.valueOf(Double.MAX_VALUE) whenever normaliseAdminTarget resolves to null (EconomyServiceImpl:164-168). For a cheap unit price (pricePerItem = 1) and that sentinel balance, the quotient overflows int. The isOwnerEconomicallyActive guard only short-circuits when there is NO server-economy account; a mis-scaled sentinel is still plausible under partial-fill config, and even a legitimately very rich owner selling a 1-priced item overflows.
recommendation: Clamp the quotient before converting, e.g. use intValue() with a Math.min against a sane max (the shop's max amount / stack count) or catch the overflow and treat it as 'more than enough' by capping at the requested item count. Never call intValueExact() on an unbounded currency÷price quotient in a main-thread trade path.

description: In the SHOP_DEPOSIT_FAILED branch the owner is notified with the message key 'chestshop.CLIENT_DEPOSIT_FAILED' (the client-side failure text), not a shop/owner-appropriate key. The owner receives a message that misstates whose deposit failed. The client separately gets the correct SHOP_DEPOSIT_FAILED key. This is a misleading error message — the owner is told the *client's* deposit failed when the failure is on the shop/owner side.
recommendation: Send the owner an owner-oriented key (e.g. a dedicated 'your shop could not receive payment' message) rather than reusing chestshop.CLIENT_DEPOSIT_FAILED, so the notification names the correct side of the failed deposit.
